  "decision": "fail",

  "findings": [
    "Skirtboard gap wear exceeds allowable limits, creating a nip point and potential for material spillage.",
    "Worn skirtboard gap compromises conveyor sealing, increasing risk of entrapment and fugitive material."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately lock out and tag out the conveyor section.",
    "Replace or adjust the skirtboard to restore proper gap and sealing per manufacturer specifications.",
    "Inspect adjacent conveyor components for secondary damage or wear."
  ],
  "rationale": "Observed skirtboard gap wear at rough-complete stage indicates the component is worn and non-compliant. Per OSHA 1910.212, machinery must have guards to protect against nip points; a worn skirtboard gap creates an unguarded hazard. CEMA standards require proper skirtboard sealing to prevent material escape and ensure safe operation. The condition poses a high risk of operator injury and equipment damage, necessitating immediate correction before acceptance.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.212(a)(1) - Machine guarding",
    "CEMA Standard 575 - Bulk Material Belt Conveyor Impact Bed/Cradle",
    "CEMA Belt Conveyors for Bulk Materials, Chapter 7 - Skirtboards"
  ]
}
